failure_surface: Test case failed (test_case_results: [0]). The task required formulas in 'Sheet1'!I3:L7 range but formulas were only written to J3:L6, missing column I entirely.
root_cause: The agent misread the answer_position specification ('Sheet1'!I3:L7) and incorrectly targeted J3:L6 instead. Additionally, the agent introduced a Python f-string syntax error when attempting dynamic formula construction, then reverted to static hardcoded ranges without verifying if the target range matched the instruction requirements.
minimal_fix_hypothesis: Before writing any formulas, extract and verify the exact target cell range from the answer_position field. Parse the range correctly (including both start and end columns), and write formulas to cover the entire specified range, not just part of it.
evidence: answer_position field explicitly states: 'Sheet1'!I3:L7 (columns I through L, rows 3 through 7)
Agent's solution code writes only to ws[f'J{row}'], ws[f'K{row}'], ws[f'L{row}'] for rows 3-6, omitting column I and row 7
First attempt contained f-string syntax error: ws[f'J{row}'] = f'=SUMPRODUCT($C$3:$C$6,{D$row}:{D$row})/$C$3:$C$6)'
Agent acknowledged row 7 already had simple SUM/AVERAGE formulas but chose to write only to rows 3-6 instead of following the full specified range
Evaluation result shows complete failure (test_case_results: [0]) indicating output did not match expected state

memory_items.title: Parse Full Target Range Before Writing
memory_items.lesson: Always parse both the starting and ending cell references from the answer_position specification. The range defines the complete area to modify, not just a subset.
memory_items.generalization: When given a range like 'A1:D10', calculate the full span: from column A to D and row 1 to 10. Write operations must cover this entire extent.

memory_items.title: Validate Formula Syntax Before Execution
memory_items.lesson: Excel formula strings passed via Python must not contain invalid syntax in string interpolation. Use escaped quotes or template strings carefully to avoid f-string errors within formula construction.
memory_items.generalization: Build formula strings using safe concatenation or format methods. Test that the resulting string is valid Excel syntax before assigning to cells.

memory_items.title: Respect Specified Boundaries Exactly
memory_items.lesson: Do not assume partial coverage of a range is acceptable. If the task specifies I3:L7, ensure all columns I through L and all rows 3 through 7 receive the required manipulation.
memory_items.generalization: Compare your code's loop bounds against the explicit range dimensions. Adjust iterations to include both endpoints (min/max row and column).

edits.content:
## 6. Range Parsing & Boundary Validation
- **Full Coverage**: When a range is specified (e.g., A1:D5), calculate all columns from start to end letter and all rows from start to end number. Operations must cover every cell in this bounding box.
- **Range Extraction**: Always extract both the starting cell and ending cell from instructions. Never assume a default width or height; compute loops based on `end_row - start_row + 1` and `end_col - start_col + 1`.
- **Formula String Safety**: Build Excel formula strings using safe string formatting methods. Avoid embedding special characters directly in f-strings that could cause syntax errors. Test formula strings are valid before assignment.
